title: Australia's Former PM Scott Morrison Pushes For More "Indo" In Indo-Pacific Agenda
content: Former Australian Prime Minister Scott Morrison argued that removing Iran's capability for a nuclear attack was justified, and said he was not surprised the conflict had entered another active phase

title: Become An "Expert Mother" First, Then IAS Officer: UP Governor To Women
content: Anandiben Patel urged young women to contribute to the well-being of their families and society as they continue their education and careers after marriage.

title: Bullying, Call For Help: CCTV Shows Moments Before Jaipur Schoolgirl's Death
content: Amaira's family has alleged inaction on the part of the police and has called for stricter provisions of the Juvenile Justice (JJ) Act to be applied, not only against the class teacher but also against the school's principal and chairman.

title: BJP Inducts 3 Ex-Trinamool MPs, Fields Them For Rajya Sabha Polls
content: The BJP's Central Election Committee announced their names for the July 24 Rajya Sabha bypolls, in what appears a 180-degree shift in their policy of not accepting Trinamool leaders in the party.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 After US Scrutiny, CEO Says "Made Many Changes"
content: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 offerings and other cutting-edge AI models, including Anthropic's Mythos series, have drawn concern over what researchers have described as an unprecedented ability to identify weaknesses in code that hackers can exploit.

title: 6 Arrested As Wildlife Trafficking Racket Busted, 53 Animals Rescued
content: The joint operation, carried out with the support of the Wildlife Crime Control Bureau (WCCB), led to the rescue of 15 slow lorises, two binturongs, 28 star tortoises, six Egyptian vultures and two shikra birds.

title: 10 Stations, 7 Lines: Delhi Metro Begins 12-km Expansion Work
content: The DMRC on Thursday started the construction of the 12.377-km Inderlok-Indraprastha corridor that will run through Central Delhi.

title: "State Of Instability": Congress Stirs Pot After Sharad Pawar-E Shinde Meet
content: "Five or six MPs from Sharad Pawar's NCP are restless. It is unclear what they will do, but they are currently in a state of instability," senior Congress leader and former Maharashtra Chief Minister Prithviraj Chavan said.

title: 'US Jobs Are For Americans, Not Foreign Fraudsters': Vance Amid H-1B Probe
content: Vance said H-1B work visa programme was originally a route for "a brilliant technology person, or a brilliant scientist, or a brilliant doctor" to work legally in the United States.

title: Israel Threatens To Attack Iran "With Greater Force" Amid US Strikes
content: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran had been weakened by the two previous military campaigns Israel launched against it.

title: Putin May Escalate Ukraine War Despite Peace Efforts By Trump: Report
content: President Vladimir Putin is rejecting calls to negotiate peace with Kyiv, with Ukraine's recent drone strikes on Russia's oil refineries and ports strengthening his resolve to keep fighting for now.

title: NDTV Investigation: Why Did 8 New Mothers Suddenly Fall Ill At A Jodhpur Hospital?
content: Part 2 of NDTV's investigation into the series of maternal deaths and severe post-delivery complications reported across government hospitals in Rajasthan focuses on Jodhpur.

title: Delhi's Major Flooding Hotspots Avoid Waterlogging Amid Heavy Rain
content: While heavy rain led to waterlogging and traffic disruptions in parts of the capital, an NDTV ground check found that several of Delhi's chronic flood-prone corridors, including Minto Bridge, Dhaula Kuan underpass, Moolchand underpass, remained free of waterlogging and traffic continued to move.

title: 3 Former Trinamool Rajya Sabha MPs Join BJP In Bengal
content: State BJP president Samik Bhattacharya welcomed them to the party at its West Bengal headquarters in Salt Lake.

title: 3 Bihar Workers Killed After SUV Hits Them In UP's Jewar
content: The accident occurred on Wednesday night near Neemka village on the Jewar-Khurja road, within the Jewar police station limits, they said.

title: Why India's Uranium Deal With Australia Could Transform Its Nuclear Future
content: India's installed nuclear capacity currently stands at around 8 GW, but the government's vision is to raise that figure to 100 GW by 2047.

title: Tihar food 'too spicy, oily, deep-fried': US mercenary seeks chicken, fish in court plea
content: Matthew Aaron VanDyke, arrested by NIA, seeks court permission to prepare his own meals. He claims jail food is too oily and spicy, causing health issues. VanDyke has reportedly lost significant weight since May 6. He requests specific cooking items and food supplies for his dietary needs. The court has issued notices to jail authorities and the NIA for their response.

title: Target vs target: What US hit in Iran, and what Tehran struck in response
content: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has collapsed after fresh military strikes. Both nations launched attacks, expanding the conflict across the Gulf region. Iran targeted US air defence systems and facilities in several Middle Eastern countries. The United States struck approximately ninety military sites across Iran. These exchanges marked the biggest breach of the truce since April.

title: J&K orders screening of books with ‘anti-national’ content, opposition slams move
content: J&K authorities are reviewing books in educational institutions for objectionable content. This action follows the withdrawal of books describing Kashmir as occupied territory. Political leaders have criticized the review as an attempt to control historical narratives. Schools must submit compliance reports by July nineteenth confirming book reviews. A legal group has also sought an inquiry into these controversial books.

title: 'American jobs ought to go to American workers': JD Vance as US cracks down on H-1B fraud
content: US Vice President JD Vance has declared a strict crackdown on suspected abuses of the H-1B visa program. The Department of Labor has commenced investigations and served subpoenas to various organizations. This initiative aims to safeguard taxpayer funds and curb any misuse of the visa system.

title: Salesforce staff 'confused' as it promotes Claude in $27.7 bn app it owns
content: Salesforce is promoting Anthropic's new Claude Tag AI agent inside Slack—the platform it bought for $27.7 billion—leaving employees confused, The Information reports. Claude Tag competes directly with Salesforce's own Slackbot and Agentforce, blurring the line between Salesforce's AI products and its partner's. With a 1% Anthropic stake and $300 million in annual token spending, Salesforce backs a rival that may not need Slack at all.

title: FSSAI issues notices to alcoholic beverage manufacturers for misleading claims
content: The FSSAI has issued notices to alcoholic beverage manufacturers for alleged regulatory violations.  Manufacturers are accused of using prohibited added flavors and making misleading age claims.  These actions contravene established Food Safety and Standards Regulations for alcoholic beverages.  Companies must now ensure compliance and provide explanations for their practices.  Failure to comply may result in further regulatory action being initiated against them.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: Landslide hits Vaishno Devi track at Himkoti; battery car service suspended
content: Intense rainfall caused a landslide near Himkoti on the new pilgrimage track. This incident prompted the temporary suspension of the battery car service on the route. The old yatra route remained unaffected, and devotees continued their journey on the traditional path. Shrine board authorities are working to clear the debris and monitor the area. Yatris are advised to follow official advisories and check travel updates.

title: One Walmart heiress owns $300M yacht, another has a giant Texas ranch
content: Walmart heiresses Ann Walton Kroenke and Nancy Walton Laurie are icons of immense wealth, each showcasing their lifestyle choices through extravagant assets. While Ann is the proud owner of the sprawling Waggoner Ranch, one of the largest in the U.S., Nancy indulges in her luxurious $300 million superyacht, Kaos, which has drawn the ire of environmental activists.

title: World’s oldest fortresses that have stood strong for thousands of years
content: Ancient fortresses stand as enduring monuments of strength and engineering.  These structures were built to safeguard kingdoms and repel invading forces.  Masada, Citadel of Aleppo, and Arg-e Bam showcase remarkable ancient architecture.  Mycenae Citadel and Erebuni Fortress reveal insights into early civilizations.  Acrocorinth demonstrates formidable natural defenses and strategic importance.

title: Mark Zuckerberg returns to Elon Musk's X after three years, announces latest AI model
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the Muse Spark 1.1 AI model's public developer access.  This marks Meta's shift towards paid enterprise offerings, challenging rivals.  The advanced model excels at coding and autonomous digital operations.  Developers can now build applications using Muse Spark via a new API.  Early US users receive free trial credits before a pay-as-you-go model.

title: Army chief visits forward areas along LoC in Poonch-Rajouri
content: Army chief General Dhiraj Seth visited forward areas along the Line of Control. He reviewed the region's security situation and counter-infiltration grid. General Seth also assessed operational preparedness and combat readiness of troops. He interacted with soldiers deployed at forward posts to boost morale. The visit occurred amid heightened security for the ongoing Amarnath Yatra.

title: Farooq invites BJP, casts wide net for J&K statehood protest
content: The National Conference has invited various political parties for a protest.  This demonstration is scheduled for July 20 at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi.  The protest aims to demand the restoration of statehood for Jammu and Kashmir.  Invitations have been extended to leaders from across the political spectrum.  The event coincides with the opening day of Parliament's monsoon session.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for July 10, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 10, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Red Wings emerge as surprise landing spot for Blue Jackets' $57.5M star
content: The Columbus Blue Jackets’ star player Zach Werenski recently vetoed a confirmed trade to the Dallas Stars as he blocked the deal with his no move clause, ahead of the new season. But the winning defenseman has also refused to sign a long term extension with his team. Amid all the mess, the Detroit Red Wings have emerged as a potential landing spot even as the team deals with the drama around Dylan Larkin.

title: Taylor Swift's heartfelt school note sparks jealousy debate among fans
content: An alleged yearbook note from Taylor Swift to a high school classmate has gone crazy viral online. Many commenters interpret the heartfelt note as a sign of romantic feelings and jealousy. Others argue it reflects common language among close female friends who are now millennials. They state such expressions were typical teenage demeanor in the early 2000s. The widespread discussion highlights differing views on female platonic admiration for one another.

title: Conor McGregor reveals romantic change ahead of UFC 329 clash
content: Conor McGregor has revealed that he made a major personal change during his training camp ahead of his long-awaited return at UFC 329. The former two-division UFC champion said he chose to avoid all physical intimacy while preparing for his rematch against Max Holloway, believing the decision could help improve his performance inside the Octagon.

title: MHA unveils four-layer 'smart border' grid, cites J&K, Northeast gains
content: The government is establishing a robust four-layer security grid to fortify India's borders. This initiative aims to create a modern border security system for the nation. Efforts are underway to combat drug trafficking and achieve victory over the menace. The government is also working to prevent illegal infiltration and demographic changes. This proactive approach strengthens national security and border region prosperity.

title: 'We don't wear rose-tinted glasses': Russia reacts to US Patriot missile licence for Ukraine
content: Russia acknowledges continued US weapons supply to Ukraine after a new license.  Moscow believes the United States desires a peace process, despite military aid.  Russia rejects the idea that deeper Ukrainian strikes could end the conflict.  Such actions will prolong the war and expand Russia's security buffer zone.  The Kremlin views the US position as dualistic regarding peace and escalation.

title: US man charged after cutting off private part, setting it ablaze in neighbour's garage
content: A man faces charges after allegedly cutting off his private body part. He then doused the severed body part with gasoline and set it ablaze. The incident occurred inside a neighbor's garage on May sixth. Investigators recovered a knife, lighters, and gasoline from the scene. The case remains under active investigation by authorities.

title: Union minister Ravneet Bittu calls Diljit Dosanjh 'impostor' amid Satluj row
content: The controversy over Diljit Dosanjh's film Satluj has taken a fresh political turn. Union Minister Ravneet Singh Bittu has called the actor-singer an "impostor" while denying allegations that the Centre had anything to do with the film being taken down from Zee5. Speaking in Ludhiana, Bittu said the government has no control over OTT platforms and dismissed claims that political pressure led to the film's removal.

title: Shreyas surpasses Rohit, equals Kohli in unique captaincy record
content: Shreyas Iyer equalled Virat Kohli's record of six consecutive toss wins as India captain in T20Is and is now one shy of MS Dhoni's record of seven. Despite Iyer's luck at the toss, India have lost their last two matches to England. Injuries to Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy have further dented India's hopes ahead of the final two T20Is.

title: Dombivli hospital row: 2 doctors assaulted by Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre quit jobs
content: Two doctors resigned after an alleged assault by Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre. Protests erupted across Maharashtra's medical fraternity following the traumatic incident. The accused corporator was admitted to Thane District Civil Hospital after his arrest. Doctors demanded his disqualification and stronger security measures for healthcare professionals. The incident has reignited concerns over the safety of healthcare workers nationwide.

title: Why India's housing market is in consolidation phase; premium demand to drive H2
content: India's housing market is expected to remain stable through late 2026. Demand is supported by urbanisation and infrastructure expansion, while growth moderates. Premium homes are driving market growth, and affordable housing faces pressure. Developers may use incentives as launches outpace buyer absorption. Continued investment and stable employment will keep the market resilient.

title: 'Not the only one who failed': Coach says Samson unfairly singled out after Zimbabwe snub
content: Sanju Samson's coach Biju George expressed disappointment over his omission from the Zimbabwe T20Is. George stated Samson deserved more opportunities after his World Cup heroics. He questioned the selectors' decisions regarding player treatment and opportunities. Samson's past junior career also saw similar perceived unfairness. The coach hopes Samson proves his critics wrong with future performances.

title: Flight delayed or cancelled due to heavy rain: Know how to get your refund
content: Monsoon weather disrupts flights, but passenger rights are protected. Airlines offer refunds or alternative flights for cancellations due to heavy rain. Passengers may receive meals and accommodation during significant flight delays. Refunds are processed within fourteen working days after a request is submitted. Passengers can escalate issues through AirSewa or consumer commissions if needed.

title: How to get Holofoil Sprite in Fortnite: Location, ability, and more
content: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 introduces Holofoil Sprites which boost rare item discovery chances.  These companions offer a five percent increase to finding rarer Sprite variants.  Players can find these Sprites near Golden Fishing Spots and specific map locations.  A special Holofoil Hours event on July 11, 2026, will increase spawn rates.  This event also provides players with a Self-Revive Device and Shock Rock effects.

title: Hours after joining BJP, ex-TMC leaders named Rajya Sabha bypoll candidates
content: Three former Trinamool Congress leaders have joined the Bharatiya Janata Party. They were immediately nominated as BJP candidates for upcoming Rajya Sabha bypolls. These by-elections are for seats that fell vacant after their resignations last month. Their move follows internal turmoil and a significant defeat for the TMC. The BJP aims to strengthen its West Bengal presence with their experience.

title: 'New Natwarlal spending Rs 160cr on aircraft': Rebel MLA Ritabrata after HC relief to TMC
content: A rebel Trinamool Congress MLA will approach the Supreme Court regarding frozen party bank accounts. The Calcutta High Court allowed limited operation of these accounts for routine expenses. Ritabrata Banerjee alleges money laundering in aircraft and helicopter purchases using party funds. He plans to submit a complaint to the Enforcement Directorate in Delhi. The TMC denies all allegations, calling the probe politically motivated.

title: Bihar mob allegedly forces cousins to perform 'marriage' rituals, remove burqa
content: Police arrested two men in Nawada, Bihar, after a viral video showed a couple being harassed. The accused allegedly forced a woman to remove her veil and her companion to apply sindoor. The incident occurred on July 8 when the pair stopped to rest near Madhopur village. Investigators identified the suspects by verifying the motorcycle registration number from the video.

title: 3-year-old drowns, car falls into sinkhole as heavy rain batters Noida-Ghaziabad
content: A three-year-old girl tragically died after slipping into rainwater outside her Ghaziabad home. Heavy monsoon rains caused widespread waterlogging and traffic disruptions across Noida and Ghaziabad. A residential road in Vasundhara Sector 13 collapsed into a large pit after heavy rainfall. Several parts of Noida experienced knee-deep water, paralyzing normal daily activities. The incidents highlighted significant shortcomings in the civic infrastructure and monsoon preparedness.

title: Monsoon covers entire India, overall rain deficit reduced to 14%
content: The entire nation of India is now experiencing monsoon conditions, which has helped in mitigating the previous rainfall deficit. Unfortunately, ten states are still grappling with considerable shortages of rain, hampering kharif crop sowing operations. This year, kharif crop acreage is down by twenty-one percent when compared to last year. Moreover, some areas may soon face another reduction in rainfall activity as predictions indicate limited respite ahead.

title: 10 red cards! Argentine referee for France-Morocco game once became players' nightmare
content: Argentina's Facundo Tello will referee the France-Morocco World Cup quarter-final, drawing attention for his strict officiating style after famously showing 10 red cards in an Argentine Super Cup match. FIFA's decision to appoint an all-Argentine refereeing team has sparked debate, but France's players dismissed concerns, insisting they are focused solely on preparing for Morocco.

title: Strait of Hormuz toll proposal: Which major waterways charge ships to transit
content: The possibility of imposing transit charges in the Strait of Hormuz has drawn fresh attention to the dependence of global supply chains on a small number of strategically important waterways.

title: Setback for Mamata Banerjee: Three former TMC MPs join BJP in Kolkata
content: Three former Trinamool Congress leaders formally joined the Bharatiya Janata Party in Kolkata. Their induction follows weeks of growing rebellion and internal turmoil within the TMC party. Sushmita Dev cited political and personal reasons for her departure from the TMC. Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Prakash Chik Baraik also resigned from their Rajya Sabha seats. The TMC faces intensified political crisis with reports of more defections fueling speculation.

title: Mumbai's rain has entered uncharted territory. The data tells a worrying story
content: Mumbai experienced unprecedented rainfall, exceeding normal monthly totals in just one week. This extreme weather reflects climate change altering rainfall patterns across India. Warmer air and seas contribute to more intense and concentrated rain events. Urban development and infrastructure limits exacerbate flooding issues in the city. Adapting to these changing monsoons requires smarter planning and natural buffer protection.

title: Mbappe, Ronaldo, and Trump: The Good, the Sad, and the Ugly
content: World Cup 2026 has given us late winners, old gods, new monsters, bad exits, ugly controversies and now France vs Morocco: a quarter-final with La Marseillaise, Moroccan drums, PSG betrayals and unfinished business from 2022.

title: Video shows jet escorting slain Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei's coffin to Mashhad
content: The coffin of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been returned to Iran from Iraq following solemn funeral rites held in both Najaf and Karbala. His arrival in Mashhad drew large crowds as the community prepared for his burial at the Imam Reza shrine. Khamenei passed away on February 28 during a US-Israel operation, and current tensions continue with strikes exchanged between Iran and the US.

title: Assault on youths highlights growing vigilantism in Perumbavoor amid anti-drug crackdown
content: Three youths were reportedly publicly tried, assaulted, stripped, and forcibly tonsured by a mob that accused them of supplying ganja to a schoolboy. Six persons have been arrested in connection with the incident

title: Building tilt prompts Kochi Corpn. to seek mandatory licence
content: The move comes in the wake of a recent incident in which a two-storey building, while being lifted, tilted onto an adjacent commercial structure along M.G. Road

title: Panchayat in Kannur launches volunteer disaster response force
content: The initiative aims to ensure rapid rescue operations, reduce casualties, and strengthen community preparedness. The local body has also initiated a comprehensive safety literacy campaign

title: Street vendors’ organisations step up stir against Kannur Corporation’s ‘eviction drives’
content: The protesters demand the Corporation to halt eviction drives and implement the Street Vendors Act by surveying vendors and issuing identity cards to eligible traders

title: Greens demand immediate cancellation of Wayanad tunnel project
content: The protesters seek legal action against the contracting company, its blacklisting from future projects, and the filing of criminal charges against it

title: Heavy rain brings down 150-year-old clock tower at Kozhikode railway station
content: The collapse sent debris and roofing sheets crashing onto Platform No. 2 and the overhead traction wires. While no casualties were reported, train operations remained disrupted

title: Sudha Kongara suppressed receipt of ₹4.12 crore from 2D Entertainment for Parasakthi, Dawn Pictures tells Madras HC
content: Justice K. Kumaresh Babu reserves orders on the plea to restrain the release of Idhayam Murali after filing of a counter affidavit by the production house

title: Two former Ministers skip review meetings chaired by Palaniswami
content: P. Balakrishna Reddy of Hosur and K.P. Anbalagan of Palacode were absent at the review sessions held for Krishnagiri and Dharmapuri districts respectively

title: Report abnormal population growth in border areas: Shah to SPs
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah says the government is committed to curb “unnatural demographic growth” caused by abnormal factors with a “ruthless approach”

title: Rescue mission enters final phase in Wayanad; three more bodies recovered
content: Two of the three victims whose bodies were recovered on Thursday have been identified as Rahul Sharma, an engineer from Himachal Pradesh, and Mohammad Imran, an excavator operator from Bihar.

title: Munnar to host ‘Rain 40' football tournament on August 9
content: Thirty-two teams will participate in the penalty shootout tournament — 16 from Idukki district and the rest from other districts

title: PM Modi in Australia updates: India, Australia seal landmark pacts on nuclear energy and defence
content: India and Australia decided to work expeditiously to firm up the proposed Comprehensive Economic Cooperation Agreement as well as a bilateral investment protection framework to further enhance the trade and investment relations.

title: Panel to be formed soon to examine liquor retail models in other States, says the Minister for Prohibition and Excise
content: The committee will study the functioning of excise departments in Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha, including liquor procurement, retail operations, and employee service conditions, says K. Vignesh

title: Bankipur bypoll: voters say the battle will be between BJP and JSP
content: Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor, who is in the fray, has been busy campaigning in the constituency; voters in the BJP stronghold say he will cut into the BJP’s votes

title: Patient collapses and dies after waiting in queue at Neyyattinkara hospital; Health Minister orders inquiry
content: The patient, Rajeshkumar of Koottapana, Neyyattinkara had come to the hospital around 6.30 p.m. complaining of chest pain, but the security staff allegedly did not permit him to see the doctor without an OP ticket

title: Modi, Albanese call on all parties to ‘exercise restraint’ and de-escalate tensions in the Gulf
content: PMs say maritime security cooperation is key to peaceful Indo-Pacific; welcome deal for long-term uranium exports from Australia; reiterates “strong support” for India’s membership in Nuclear Suppliers’ Group

title: India, Australia strike deal on uranium exports, deepen ties across nuclear and maritime sectors
content: The agreement on civil nuclear energy to facilitate the commercial supply of uranium from Australia to India to fuel New Delhi's nuclear power projects came nearly 12 years after the two countries inked a historic civil nuclear cooperation pact

title: Bishnoi ran decentralised syndicate, wanted ‘S.K.’ killed to instill fear: U.S. DoJ
content: DoJ says Bishnoi’s group “enticed impoverished minors in India to join the enterprise” and loyal members were sent abroad to work for the network; gang members had “limited information on the identities or backgrounds” of others in the group

title: Three former Trinamool Congress MPs join BJP, nominated to Rajya Sabha
content: Sushmita Dev, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Prakash Chik Baraik have joined the saffron party; all three leaders warmly welcomed by West Bengal BJP chief; with the BJP having 208 legislators in the 294-member Assembly, their election is just a matter of time

title: Madhya Pradesh Congress chief’s brother detained in drug case in Indore; Congress slams BJP govt
content: According to the police, Kulbhushan and Manav Gangwani were detained for questioning after their names surfaced during the interrogation of two men who were arrested with 10.8 grams of narcotic substance on Thursday

title: Baruipur encounter death: CID to probe, family refuses to accept body of rape-accused
content: Prabhas Mondal’s mother refused to claim her son’s body, saying he had met the consequences of his actions; Opposition leaders slam encounter, say Suvendu Adhikari is imitating Yogi Adityanath

title: Young Indians Thiruvananthapuram Chapter to host model parliament on July 10 and 11
content: A total of 129 selected students from various government and private schools across the city and its surrounding areas will participate in the event

title: Dwindling Fortunes: quick-commerce platforms, large retail stores sound the death knell for small businesses, neighbourhood shops in Kerala
content: Neighbourhood shops and small retailers across the country are finding the going tough with e-commerce platforms and corporate-backed large retail stores rapidly establishing their business even in rural areas. A walk along the commercial areas as well as neighbourhoods reveals the plight of those who have been struggling to cope in the digital era. S.R. Praveen meets some of the retailers to understand the changing retail business scenario in Kerala

title: Mohana Kumar appointed as State Project Director of Samagra Shiksha Kerala
content: Dr. Mohana Kumar, who was previously the principal of DB College, Sasthamcotta, had also served as the vice-chairperson of SCOLE Kerala and secretary of the Higher Secondary Education Board

title: Farooq Abdullah writes to INDIA bloc, J&K leaders on upcoming protest over Statehood
content: The NC president says: “We hope every democratic voice across the country will stand united in this collective cause”

title: Watch: India and Australia Deepen Defence Ties: What It Means for the Indo-Pacific | Above the Fold | 09.07.2026
content: From Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Australia to the escalating conflict in West Asia, to new developments in the Sri Lanka prison clashes, we bring you the latest in this episode. We also look at the Navy’s Drishti-10 UAV that crashed near Gujarat, the controversy surrounding Argentina’s latest FIFA World Cup win, and other top national and international stories. We bring you your complete news roundup.

title: Kerala HC to hear suo motu proceedings on July 2024 landslide in Wayanad
content: The proceedings had been initiated in August 2024, when cases connected with quarrying and construction activities in Wayanad came up for hearing

title: SC cites blazes in Delhi, Lucknow; questions civic bodies on unauthorised buildings
content: Bench says “slackness” by the civic authorities to take effective action against unauthorised constructions in the national capital and its neighbouring regions will not be tolerated

title: Monsoon tracker highlights | Heavy monsoon rain wreaks havoc across India, disrupts normal life
content: IMD said that there has been a delay of a day in the monsoon, covering the whole country, as it normally takes place on July 8

title: Bengaluru Assembly segments lag in enumeration form distribution as Karnataka SIR reaches 82% coverage
content: Among the Assembly constituencies with lower distribution rates, Bommanahalli recorded the lowest progress at 35.01%, followed by Bangalore South (39.95%) and Govindarajanagar (40.25%)

title: Minister moots raising retirement age of professors in govt. medical colleges
content: Their retirement age currently is 63 while it is 70 in private medical colleges; out of 800 sanctioned professor posts in 17 government medical colleges across the State, around 200 remain vacant, which is affecting the quality of medical education, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: Haripad police register case over alleged murder of medical student in Uzbekistan
content: Savariya Basanth was allegedly beaten to death with a laptop by her classmate Sadarul Anam, a native of Malappuram

title: CBI files first chargesheet in Reliance Home Finance Limited case; cites ₹3,526 crore loss
content: The agency has registered seven First Information Reports against Reliance Communications Limited (RCOM), Reliance Home Finance Limited (RHFL), Reliance Commercial Finance Limited (RCFL) and Reliance Telecom Limited (RTL) based on complaints received from various public sector banks and LIC

title: Dems lose Maine attraction; scandal scarred candidate quits Senate race
content: Platner, 41, ran as an anti-establishment progressive with the backing of senator Bernie Sanders . he defeated more established Democratic figures to become the party's nominee against Republican Senator Susan Collins . the race is crucial because Maine is one of the Democrats' best opportunities to flip a GOP-held Senate seat .

title: Punjab assembly elections 2027: AAP vs who — Congress or BJP?
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More
